parts.text:
Investigate prior-session provenance for this question without loading all transcripts into parent session.

Question: Why does src/auth/queue.ts deduplicate refresh work per tenant instead of using one global mutex?
Expected relevant prior-session title: Auth refresh queue rationale
Ignore distractor sessions related to billing retry and markdown parser.

Required workflow, in order:
1. Use session_lookup to search for the expected relevant prior-session title/keywords.
2. Use session_detail with detail='messages' on the relevant session/blob to inspect message summaries/ids.
3. Use message_detail for one exact message that contains the rationale/evidence.
4. Answer with exact identifiers: session_id, blob_id, message_id, and a concise answer explaining why per-tenant dedupe is used instead of one global mutex. Also list ignored irrelevant contexts by name if encountered.

Return only the necessary facts to the parent; do not include full transcript content.
Searching prior sessions for the specified auth refresh queue rationale and related keywords.

parts.text:
session_id: `ses_1e52561d3ffezumemo8mcjb2TT`  
blob_id: `auth_queue`  
message_id: `msg_e1ada9e38001wtgrW5JDTLwBXE`

Per-tenant dedupe is used so duplicate refreshes for the same tenant coalesce into one refresh, while unrelated tenants can refresh in parallel. A global mutex was rejected because `auth_refresh_different_tenants_parallel` showed different tenants must not block each other.

Ignored irrelevant contexts: `Billing retry queue rationale`, `Markdown parser onboarding cleanup`.
